content:
Jessica Urzúa studies how changes in organizational and categorical boundaries—through firm restructuring, legal reform, and shifts in employment relationships—produce and sustain inequality in labor markets.
She is a PhD student in Sociology and Social Policy at Harvard University. She received an AB in Social Anthropology from Harvard College and an MA in Economics from Columbia University.
summary:
Jessica Urzúa studies how changes in organizational and categorical boundaries—through firm restructuring, legal reform, and shifts in employment relationships—produce and sustain inequality in labor markets.

title: Jessica Urzúa
